title: Boolean Cut and Fuse (Add) with target-BodyId preservation
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:486-541 — Test 9 'Attached sketch extrude Add targets host body': 20x20x10 box, then Add(Fuse) extrude of a 4x4 pad distance 4 -> volume grows (updated > base + 1.0); host body id reused.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:538 — assert updated volume > baseVolume + 1.0 (Fuse grows body).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:543-597 — Test 10 'Attached sketch extrude Cut targets host body': same box, Cut extrude distance -4 -> volume shrinks (updated < base - 1.0); explicit targetBodyId reused.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:594 — assert updated volume < baseVolume - 1.0 (Cut removes material).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:525-528,582-584 — target-body resolution: Add path leaves targetBodyId empty (resolved from sketch host-face attachment); Cut path sets targetBodyId explicitly; both push the SAME bodyId into resultBodyIds (BodyId preserved through the boolean).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:930-1000 — Test 22 linear-pattern FUSE gives exact numbers: 3 solids @ 1500 (fuseResult=false) and 1 solid @ 1300 (fuseResult=true) — a clean Union-count/volume oracle.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:663-701 — Test 12 'Boolean op fails explicitly when target body is unresolved' (unresolved target => op fails, session intact).

opScript.note: Base 20x20x10 box (C++ test seeds it via doc.addBody(MakeBox(20,20,10)); volume 4000).
4x4 pad on the top face, Add/Fuse. targetBodyId empty => resolved from sketch host-face attachment (legacy fallback path). Result reuses body_1.
Independent scenario (fresh doc in C++). 4x4 pocket cut into the top face; explicit targetBodyId. Result reuses body_1.

Standalone body-body boolean schema (BooleanParams). The old regen tests exercise booleans mainly as feature-fused Add/Cut and via patterns; this op documents the standalone verb shape (SCHEMA §7.3 Boolean).

add_fuse.meaning: Fuse adds material; result body id == input body id (BodyId preserved through Add).

cut.meaning: Cut removes material; targetBodyId explicit; result body id preserved.

fuse_exact_from_pattern.note: Exact Union oracle: fuseResult toggles solidCount 3->1 and volume 1500->1300. Best hard number for boolean-Fuse correctness.

unresolved_target_rejection.newStackMapping: worker terminal resp error.code REF_UNRESOLVED (SCHEMA §8) — recoverable, NOT NeedsRepair.

notes: target-BodyId preservation is the invariant under test: Add and Cut both keep the operand body's id (plan: 'BodyId registry + lifecycle' owned by Rust). Old ids embed BodyId; new stack keeps BodyId as the body handle but element ids become body-independent. The pattern-fuse pair gives the only exact boolean volumes; Tests 9/10 assert only direction-of-change (grow/shrink).
